annotations.summary: Real-Grafana validation of alert-replay simulator scenario ① — 'Fires, never resolves' (3-node DAG: range → reduce(mean) → threshold)
annotations.description:
If you can read this email, scenario ① of the alert-replay simulator fired in real Grafana with the same params it uses to mock the lifecycle.

This alert is the live companion to grafana/tools/alert-replay scenario ▶① — 'Fires, never resolves'. The simulator predicts: with the params below, mean(rate samples)>0.05 holds continuously, the alert transitions Normal → Pending (after one wait-tick) → Alerting (after the for-window), and never returns to Normal because the underlying condition keeps holding.

Parameters — must match the simulator one-for-one:
  rateWindow      = [2m]    (PromQL inner)
  relativeTimeRange = 4m → 0s
  reducer         = mean    (NOT max — scenario ① uses mean)
  step / intervalMs = 15s   → 17 samples per evaluation (4m ÷ 15s + 1)
  threshold       = 0.05
  for             = 2m      (debounce window in Pending)
  trigger.interval = 1m     (rule evaluation cadence)

DAG (same shape as the slide-45 demo, retained because range mode + scalar threshold needs an explicit reduce node):
  A — query node, range=true, [2m] rate over 4m relativeTimeRange → time series
  B — expression node, type=reduce, reducer=mean → scalar
  C — expression node, type=threshold, gt 0.05 (source: true) → 0/1

Expected lifecycle in production (real QRC traffic is 0.29-0.83 req/s, well above the 0.05 threshold):
  t=0       — Normal
  t≈0:30+   — first eval where mean crosses 0.05 → Pending starts
  t≈2:30+   — for-window (2m) expires → Alerting → email sent
  t=∞       — never resolves; mean stays > 0.05 because traffic continues

This is the simulator's scenario ① 'delay = 2:30 = 30s wait-tick + 2:00 for-window' line, validated against real Grafana evaluation. If the actual fire time deviates more than ~30s from t≈2:30, the simulator's mock and the real engine have diverged and the simulator needs a fix.

Go template values (preserved from previous demo):
  $value             = {{ $value }}
  $labels.severity   = {{ $labels.severity }}
  $labels.job        = {{ $labels.job }}
  $labels.alertname  = {{ $labels.alertname }}

Routing:
  spec.labels { severity=warning, job=qrc-uat }
    → NotificationPolicy matcher (qrc-uat warning branch)
    → ContactPoint: David UAT Email
    → xuwei19850423@gmail.com (single recipient)

Cleanup: revert via the demo cleanup.sh once verification is done. Note the rule UID stays qrc-demo-range-max for back-compat with the cleanup/setup scripts; only the title and parameters changed.
